title: Time Sync Repair
description: Diagnose Windows time drift and synchronization errors, resync only after approval, and verify final time service state.
starter_prompt: Troubleshoot wrong system time or time sync failures with approval before repair.

phases.prompt: Scope time drift/sync symptom and collect evidence before service or resync actions.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; Section 'Local time'; Get-Date; Section 'Time service'; Get-Service W32Time -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Name,Status,StartType | Format-List; Section 'W32TM status'; w32tm /query /status 2>&1 | Out-String | Write-Output; Section 'W32TM peers'; w32tm /query /peers 2>&1 | Out-String | Write-Output; Section 'Time events'; Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{LogName='System'; ProviderName='Microsoft-Windows-Time-Service'; StartTime=(Get-Date).AddDays(-14)} -MaxEvents 100 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object TimeCreated,Id,LevelDisplayName,Message | Format-List

phases.prompt: Collect read-only time service, peer, status, and event evidence.
Analyze time sync evidence. Identify service stopped, no peers, domain policy, drift, network, or permission causes.
Ask for explicit approval before resyncing time or starting the time service. Offer restore point handling for service changes when appropriate.
phases.completion: Approval and restore point decision are recorded.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output 'Resyncing Windows time after approval.'; Start-Service W32Time -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; w32tm /resync /force 2>&1 | Out-String | Write-Output; w32tm /query /status 2>&1 | Out-String | Write-Output

phases.prompt: Run only after approval and restore point offer. Start/resync Windows Time and capture output.
phases.completion: Approved time resync completed or failure is recorded.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Get-Date; Get-Service W32Time -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Name,Status,StartType | Format-List; w32tm /query /status 2>&1 | Out-String | Write-Output

phases.prompt: Verify local time, service state, and sync status after approved action.
Check that domain-managed time policy or network failures are disclosed and not over-repaired.

Create a technician and customer-ready handoff report with evidence, findings, actions/tools used, verification status, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and prioritized follow-up next actions.
Keep logs and reports. Remove only workflow-created scratch files.

acceptance_criteria: Time service and sync evidence are collected before repair.
Resync/start action runs only after approval.
Verification captures final time and sync status.
Final report includes evidence, actions, cleanup status, risks, and follow-up items.
